extraction.one_line_summary: EWAN Caleb won a photo finish sprint in Toulouse, timing his acceleration perfectly to come around GROENEWEGEN Dylan in the final meters after appearing boxed.
tactical_patterns: Jumbo-Visma controlled early finale but lost organization inside 3km allowing Katusha to briefly take over front positioning.
Viviani repeatedly checked over shoulder in final 500m instead of committing early, suggesting uncertainty about lead-out timing.
Groenewegen launched earlier than optimal allowing Ewan time to close and come around despite appearing boxed at 200m.

rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: positive: Showed exceptional patience when appearing boxed with 200m remaining, perfectly timed acceleration against premature Groenewegen launch
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: Started final sprint too early despite having clean lead position, faded inside final 50 meters
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: Uncharacteristically hesitant in finale checking shoulders repeatedly instead of committing to sprint, finished only third
rider_signals.GREIPEL Andre: negative: Positioned far too far back at 5km in peloton tail, never able to contest despite wide uncomplicated roads
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: Took Viviani's wheel tactically but lacked top-end speed to contend for win, finished fourth
